birds.evidence: |year=2004|title=Density and habitat associations of barred owls at the edge of their range in Oklahoma|jstor=3878075|journal= Southeastern Naturalist|volume= 3|issue=3|pages= 475–482|doi= 10.1656/1528-7092(2004)003[0475:DAHAOB]2.0.CO;2 |s2cid= 85726566 }} Along the Atlantic coast area from New England to New Jersey, barred owls are often found in mixed swamps areas with cedars, seldom wandering to adjacent wetlands or farmland.{{cite journal|author1=Sutton, C
